speaker: interviewer
text: --15th of March, 1995. Lilly, did you wanna tell me what-- what this is in front of us?

speaker: interviewee
text: This is a wedding marriage certificate of my parents, in Hungarian. [LAUGHS] And-- [SIGHS]

speaker: interviewer
text: --when was that? Well, whenever-- in 1937. [POINTS] They were married.

speaker: interviewee
text: It can't be.

speaker: interviewer
text: No, no.

speaker: interviewee
text: I were-- that must have been a renewal of the certificate.

speaker: interviewer
text: Right.

speaker: interviewee
text: You know? So they renewed it in '37 for whatever reason. And I don't even know when the original was done-- no. Yeah, in 1926, [POINTS] Budapest-- thank you. They-- the 6th of June did they get married.

speaker: interviewer
text: Right.

speaker: interviewee
text: Yeah. [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. Lilly, can you tell me what these photos are?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yeah. Well, these are precious photos of mine. It's my father and my mother when they met. This is my mother's matriculation photo. And that was the-- my father, who was hired to be a pianist on the party of my mother's matriculation. And they met there. And in a way, it was a similar story than my boyfriend in Hungary, where he couldn't dance with her. So [POINTING] he asked through some-- my record player to put on, so he can dance with her. And that was it. And then they became married about a year after, or a year [INAUDIBLE], or whatever. And that was their--

speaker: interviewer
text: Wedding photo.

speaker: interviewee
text: --wedding photo. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: So that photo was taken in 1927?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yes, in 1926.

speaker: interviewee
text: Yeah-- in 1926. That was the-- the year-- marriage certificate. Now, then there were two kids born, one after the other. And that was in Varnsdorf, in that shop where I mentioned to you-- that we had a shop-- a shoe shop downstairs. And we lived upstairs.

speaker: interviewer
text: So that--

speaker: interviewee
text: That was--

speaker: interviewer
text: --was taken. That photo was taken in Varnsdorf.

speaker: interviewee
text: Yes, that was there.

speaker: interviewer
text: And who's that a photo of?

speaker: interviewee
text: For-- of Freddie and myself.

speaker: interviewer
text: Right.

speaker: interviewee
text: [POINTING] And if you look at it, like I said before, we always were dressed in exactly the same, in spite of a male and a female. But we-- and these are two ski sort of teddy bear outfits, which we had the same.

speaker: interviewer
text: And do you know when that photo was taken, the date of that photo?

speaker: interviewee
text: No. But I'll tell you. I could be around about six years old. And-- so-- it could be well, '33 or '30. [PAUSES FOR 6 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: 1933, approximately.

speaker: interviewee
text: Yeah.

speaker: interviewer
text: Mm-hmm.

speaker: interviewee
text: And later on when we came to Budapest, and my-- by that time, my father wasn't alive anymore. And that is my auntie who had--

speaker: interviewer
text: And what was her name?

speaker: interviewee
text: She-- Irma Szenes.

speaker: interviewer
text: Can you spell that for me?

speaker: interviewee
text: I-R-M-A, Irma, Szenes-- S-Z-E-N-E-S. She was pregnant with that little girl, whom I was mentioning before, on the photo she was pregnant in. Next to her is standing my grandmother, her mother. Next to grandma is my mother. So actually, grandma had two daughters.

speaker: interviewer
text: And what was grandma-- what was your grandmother's name?

speaker: interviewee
text: Elsa Weiszenstein. And my mother was Margaret Baruch [INAUDIBLE]. That was my brother, Freddie. And that's myself.

speaker: interviewer
text: And where was that photo taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: That was in the apartment in Budapest, where we all lived together. It was a very nice, modern building. And the flat was quite nice as well, as far as I remember.

speaker: interviewer
text: And do you know what year that photo was taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: Well, she-- when Irma was-- Irma, she was pregnant. So it must have been in 1941, '42.

speaker: interviewer
text: Right.

speaker: interviewee
text: '42, because she was born in '43, if I remember well.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. Lilly, can you tell me what these are?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yes, these are my two sons. The younger one is not married. He's on the left side. He's single yet, working in New York at present moment. And the older one is married. And--

speaker: interviewer
text: The one on the right, the one-- the single photo?

speaker: interviewee
text: The single photo on the right, yes.

speaker: interviewer
text: What's his name?

speaker: interviewee
text: He's Ron. And he's a good looking guy.

speaker: interviewer
text: [LAUGHS]

speaker: interviewee
text: And not that the other one is not good looking.

speaker: interviewer
text: [LAUGHS]

speaker: interviewee
text: But he-- the other one, the older one is Gerry-- Gerald, officially. And he's married. And he's got my gorgeous grandchild.

speaker: interviewer
text: Is he-- is he married to the woman in the photo?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yes, he's married to Holly, who is a lovely, lovely girl. And I'm very grateful for him to pick such a lovely girl.

speaker: interviewer
text: And so these, both your sons, now live where?

speaker: interviewee
text: In New York.

speaker: interviewer
text: And do you know when these photos were taken-- how recently?

speaker: interviewee
text: Well, one was taken-- Ron's picture was taken about-- oh, I would say about three years ago. Where--

speaker: interviewer
text: So that would be about 1992.

speaker: interviewee
text: Yes. And Gerry and Holly were visiting Sydney in 1990 for Christmas, '95. And that was our farewell dinner in a restaurant. And that was-- picture was taken there.

speaker: interviewer
text: Right. And you mentioned that Gerry and Holly now have a child.

speaker: interviewee
text: Yes. They have a child. [PAUSES FOR 5 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: [LAUGHS]

speaker: interviewee
text: And what's-- is that a-- He's Austin.

speaker: interviewer
text: Austin.

speaker: interviewee
text: He was born in '94, 30th of June.

speaker: interviewer
text: And how old is he there?

speaker: interviewee
text: Here, he's about 7 and 1/2, 8 months.

speaker: interviewer
text: Great.
